heuristics.name: Use data as the medium
heuristics.description: The dataset is the core artifact, not supporting evidence.

heuristics.name: Make hidden systems visible
heuristics.description: Expose behavior that is technically public but socially illegible.

heuristics.name: Build a one-question UX
heuristics.description: The project should answer one clear question immediately.

heuristics.name: Keep it weekend-scale
heuristics.description: Favor thin prototypes, cheap hardware, static artifacts, and commodity APIs.

heuristics.name: Make the headline the product
heuristics.description: The title, first sentence, and screenshot should explain the whole artifact.

heuristics.name: Explain how it works
heuristics.description: A methodology note, caveat, or source explanation is part of the interface.

heuristics.name: Plan for ephemerality
heuristics.description: Assume unaffiliated data sources can disappear and build snapshot mode.

heuristics.name: Treat reaction as output
heuristics.description: Press, backlash, shutdowns, and remixing reveal what the project touched.

heuristics.name: Separate publicness from permission
heuristics.description: Public data can still create harm when aggregated, ranked, or reframed.

required_safety_defaults: Avoid non-consensual biometric or attractiveness scoring
Include uncertainty notes for inferred data
Do not provide harmful scraping or evasion instructions
